target.title: diag(cli-runner): attribute AbortError trigger source
target.body:
## Problem

`[agent/cli-backend] claude live session turn failed: ... error=AbortError` log lines have no detail field, so operators cannot tell which of four distinct trigger paths fired:

1. User-typed abort word (`stop`, `wait`, etc. via `ABORT_TRIGGERS`)
2. `abortForRestart` from compaction-restart / drain
3. Mid-turn `/model` switch (`requestLiveSessionModelSwitch`)
4. Explicit `chat.abort` RPC

This makes the bug pattern impossible to fix without speculation. We've been seeing 20+ AbortError lines / 10 days across our deployment with no way to attribute them.

## Change

Diagnostic enrichment only — no behavior change.

- `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts`:
  - `createAbortError(reason?)` now accepts and includes the upstream abort reason in the error message (was: zero-arg, fixed `"CLI run aborted"`).
  - `failTurn` appends `detail="..."` (truncated to 240 chars) to the log line.
  - The two abort paths split: `abortFromSignal` reads `params.context.params.abortSignal.reason` (preserves `controller.abort(reason)` payload — `UserAbortError` for stop-words, `Error("Reply operation aborted for restart")` for compaction, etc.); `abortFromReplyBackend` tags `replyBackend:<reason>` (`user_abort`, `restart`, or `superseded`).
- `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts`: same `createCliAbortError(reason?)` upgrade for the non-live CLI path.
- `src/gateway/chat-abort.ts`: `abortChatRunById` calls `controller.abort("chat-abort:<stopReason>")` instead of `.abort()`, so the existing `stopReason` (e.g., `"rpc"`, `"stop"`, `"timeout"`) propagates.

## Effect on log lines

Before:
\`\`\`
... error=AbortError
\`\`\`

After (one of):
\`\`\`
... error=AbortError detail="CLI run aborted: AbortError: Reply operation aborted by user"
... error=AbortError detail="CLI run aborted: AbortError: Reply operation aborted for restart"
... error=AbortError detail="CLI run aborted: replyBackend:restart"
... error=AbortError detail="CLI run aborted: chat-abort:rpc"
\`\`\`

That is sufficient to attribute each abort to its real source (operator stop-word vs. compaction restart vs. model switch vs. RPC) without re-investigating from scratch.

## Tests

- New: `src/agents/cli-runner/abort-reason.test.ts` — 7 unit tests covering string / Error / object / nullish abort-reason inputs for both `createAbortError` (live-session path) and `createCliAbortError` (non-live path). All pass.
- Existing: `src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts` 4/4 pass; `src/agents/cli-runner/` 35/35 pass.
- `pnpm tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json` and `pnpm tsgo -p tsconfig.core.test.json` both clean.

`pnpm check:changed` not run — would be appreciated as part of CI verification.

## Out of scope

I deliberately did NOT raise the abort grace timeout, change `ABORT_TRIGGERS`, or alter compaction-restart policy. Without trigger evidence those would be guesses. This PR is the prerequisite — once a few new aborts arrive with attributed reasons, the actual fix becomes obvious.

## Provenance

This change was scoped and authored by a Claude Code background agent during a source-level diagnostic of multiple gateway issues. Full investigation writeup (root causes, failing call paths, what wasn't fixed and why) is in `DIAGNOSTIC.md` on the branch but not included in the diff so it doesn't pollute the repo.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +22, Docs +1. Total +26 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main rejects missing `message.content` in `parseFeishuMessageEventPayload()` before dispatch and still has later parsed-content `.trim()` paths; I did not run a live Feishu tenant replay in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(feishu): handle empty message content safely
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Feishu group or direct message events can arrive with runtime-missing `message.content`, especially around empty or mention-only messages.
- Why it matters: Some Feishu message paths assumed content was always a string and could call `.replace()` or `.trim()` before the empty-message guard ran, causing crashes such as `Cannot read properties of undefined`.
- What changed:
  - Normalize missing Feishu message content at the parser boundary before mention normalization.
  - Guard the handler preview path before `.replace()`.
  - Guard the debounce text path before `.trim()`.
  - Add regression coverage for missing `message.content` with mention metadata.
  - Add the required changelog entry.
- What did NOT change:
  - No dependency changes.
  - No permission, secret, network, CI, or code-execution surface changes.
  - No intentional change to normal non-empty Feishu message behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #66657
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Feishu message parsing and receive/dispatch paths assumed `message.content` was always present and string-like. When a runtime event had missing content plus mention metadata, content could be used before downstream optional chaining guards ran.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered empty JSON text such as `{"text":""}`, but did not cover runtime-missing `message.content` with mention metadata through the parser/mention normalization path.
- Contributing context (if known): The original guard was added near downstream `.trim()` usage, but earlier paths such as mention normalization, handler preview, and debounce could still read content first.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - A Feishu text event with runtime-missing `message.content` and mention metadata should not crash before sequential-key resolution.
  - The parser boundary should normalize missing content before mention normalization.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  - The regression directly covers the previously unsafe source-level path: `getFeishuSequentialKey()` calls `parseFeishuMessageEvent()`, which reaches content parsing and mention normalization before resolving the lane.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
  - Existing Feishu tests covered empty parsed text, but not missing runtime `message.content` with mention metadata.
- If no new test is added, why not:
  - N/A. A regression test was added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Feishu empty or mention-only messages with missing runtime content should no longer crash parsing, debounce, sequential-key resolution, or dispatch preview paths.

Expected behavior after this change:

- Missing `message.content` is normalized to an empty string at the parser boundary.
- Mention normalization receives a string.
- Handler preview does not call `.replace()` on missing content.
- Debounce does not call `.trim()` on missing content.
- Empty messages without media are skipped safely instead of crashing.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Feishu event
  -> message.content missing
  -> parseMessageContent(undefined)
  -> normalizeMentions(non-string)
  -> .replace() / .trim() crash before downstream guard

After:
Feishu event
  -> message.content missing
  -> parser boundary normalizes to ""
  -> parseMessageContent("")
  -> normalizeMentions("")
  -> sequential key / debounce / handler preview continue safely
  -> empty message is skipped when there is no text and no media
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu
- Runtime/container: Node.js 22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.2
- Integration/channel: Feishu
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A for local source-level runtime proof

### Steps

1. Create a Feishu text event.
2. Remove `event.message.content` at runtime.
3. Add mention metadata to `event.message.mentions`.
4. Pass the event through `parseFeishuMessageEvent()`.
5. Pass the same event through `getFeishuSequentialKey()`.
6. Verify the parser and mention normalization path completes without throwing.
7. Verify the stable Feishu chat lane is returned.

### Expected

- Missing `message.content` is treated as empty text.
- `normalizeMentions()` receives a string.
- `getFeishuSequentialKey()` returns the base chat lane.
- Handler preview and debounce paths are guarded against missing content.

### Actual

- Before the full fix, downstream optional chaining guarded only later `.trim()` sites, while parser/mention normalization and preview/debounce paths could still read missing content first.
- After the fix, parser-boundary normalization and defensive preview/debounce guards prevent those paths from calling string methods on missing content.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior

Feishu events with runtime-missing `message.content` and mention metadata no longer crash the Feishu parser, mention normalization, sequential-key resolution, debounce text resolution, or handler preview paths.

The fixed behavior is that missing message content is normalized to an empty string before mention normalization, then empty messages without media are safely skipped instead of causing `.replace()` or `.trim()` to run on `undefined`.

### environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu
- Runtime: Node.js 22.22.2
- Package manager: pnpm 10.33.2
- Integration/channel: Feishu
- Setup used for proof: local OpenClaw checkout running the production Feishu parser and sequential-key code with a redacted Feishu event shape
- Live Feishu tenant: not available

### steps

1. Ran the current PR branch locally after the fix.
2. Created a redacted Feishu text event with `message.content` intentionally missing.
3. Added Feishu mention metadata to the event.
4. Executed the real OpenClaw Feishu parser via `parseFeishuMessageEvent()`.
5. Executed the real sequential-key path via `getFeishuSequentialKey()`.
6. Confirmed the parser returned string content and the sequential-key path completed without throwing.

### evidence

Command run:

```text
pnpm exec tsx feishu-real-proof.ts
```

Redacted input shape:

```text
message_type: "text"
chat_type: "group"
message.content: <missing>
message.mentions:
  - key: "@_user_1"
    id.open_id: "<redacted_bot_open_id>"
    name: "<redacted>"
```

After-fix terminal output:

```text
Feishu real-behavior local proof
Input shape: {
  message_type: 'text',
  chat_type: 'group',
  hasContent: false,
  mentions: 1
}
Parsed content: ""
Parsed content type: string
Mentioned bot: true
Has any mention: true
Sequential key: feishu:default:oc_group_redacted
Result: PASS - missing message.content did not crash parser, mention normalization, or sequential-key resolution
```

Relevant after-fix parser boundary:

```ts
const rawContent = parseMessageContent(event.message.content ?? "", event.message.message_type);
```

Supplemental local validation:

```text
pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.stripBotMention.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       81 passed (81)
```

Formatter validation:

```text
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.ts extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot-content.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.stripBotMention.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.message-handler.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.account.ts CHANGELOG.md

All matched files use the correct format.
```

### observedResult

- `parseFeishuMessageEvent()` completed without throwing when `message.content` was missing.
- `parsed.content` was returned as a string.
- `normalizeMentions()` did not receive `undefined`.
- `getFeishuSequentialKey()` returned the stable Feishu chat lane.
- The missing-content + mention metadata path no longer crashes before downstream guards.
- Handler preview and debounce were updated to avoid calling string methods on missing content.

### notTested

I did not verify against a live Feishu tenant because I do not have Feishu test credentials or access to the original provider payload. The proof above uses a local OpenClaw runtime execution of the production Feishu parser and sequential-key code with redacted Feishu event data matching the missing-content shape from the issue.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Evidence provided in this PR:

- Local OpenClaw runtime execution of the production Feishu parser and sequential-key code with a redacted missing-content Feishu event.
- Regression test covering runtime-missing `message.content` with mention metadata.
- Local test output showing 81 Feishu tests passed.
- Formatter output showing all matched files use the correct format.
- Diff showing parser-boundary normalization, handler preview guard, debounce guard, and changelog entry.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Ran a local proof script against the production Feishu parser and sequential-key code.
  - Confirmed missing `message.content` with mention metadata is normalized safely.
  - Ran the Feishu regression/unit test set locally.
  - Confirmed missing `message.content` with mention metadata is covered by `sequential-key.test.ts`.
  - Confirmed parser-boundary normalization is applied before mention normalization.
  - Confirmed handler preview uses a safe `content` variable before `.replace()`.
  - Confirmed debounce guards resolved text before `.trim()`.
  - Confirmed `CHANGELOG.md` includes the Feishu fix entry.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Missing `message.content`.
  - Mention metadata present.
  - Empty content path with no media.
  - Sequential-key resolution after parser normalization.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - I did not verify a live Feishu tenant replay because I do not have Feishu test credentials or the original provider payload.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

Risk: The real provider payload shape for #66657 may differ from the source-level reproduced missing-content event.

Mitigation:
- The fix normalizes missing content at the parser boundary and adds defensive guards in preview and debounce paths, so it is intentionally tolerant of absent content.
- The regression covers the unsafe missing-content + mention metadata path identified by review.
- The local proof executes the production Feishu parser and sequential-key code with redacted missing-content event data.
- A maintainer with Feishu test credentials can still replay the original live payload if strict external runtime proof is required.

target.title: feat(plugins): let hooks prefer auth profiles
target.body:
## Summary
- add `authProfileOverride` to model-resolve hook results alongside provider/model overrides
- keep explicit user-pinned auth profiles protected from hook rotation
- preserve legacy auto auth-profile markers through queued follow-up runs so they do not become user-pinned
- update docs, changelog, and focused regression coverage

Supersedes #71862 with the same feature ported onto latest `main` and with the ClawSweeper findings fixed.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/setup.test.ts src/agents/live-model-switch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-override-wiring.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-agent-start.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm check:no-conflict-markers`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/concepts/agent-loop.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md docs/plugins/hooks.md docs/tools/plugin.md src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/live-model-switch.test.ts src/agents/live-model-switch.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/setup.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/setup.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-auth-profile.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue/state.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue/types.ts src/plugins/hook-before-agent-start.types.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-agent-start.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-override-wiring.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Trusted hooks should be able to prefer an auth profile for model routing, while explicit user-pinned profiles still win.
- Real environment tested: My production OpenClaw VPS, Asuman, updated to OpenClaw 2026.5.4 with the same auth-profile override logic applied as a production patch. Gateway, ZeroAPI, NoldoMem, and Slack were all loaded.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Verified the deployed gateway version, readiness endpoint, auth-profile override markers in the built runner, and gateway startup logs after restarting the gateway.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real VPS checks:

```text
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.4 (325df3e)

$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18789/readyz | head -c 160
{"ready":true,"failing":[],"uptimeMs":707101,"eventLoop":{"degraded":false,

$ grep auth-profile hook markers
/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/pi-embedded-X0afS0ip.js:1479: log$1.info(`[hooks] auth profile preferred as ${modelResolveOverride.authProfileOverride}`);
/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/pi-embedded-X0afS0ip.js:1821: const hookPreferredProfileId = params.authProfileIdSource === "user" ? void 0 : hookSelection.authProfileOverride?.trim() || void 0;
/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/pi-embedded-X0afS0ip.js:1822: const preferredProfileId = hookPreferredProfileId ?? (pluginHarnessOwnsTransport ? resolvePluginHarnessPreferredProfileId() : requestedProfileId);
/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/pi-embedded-X0afS0ip.js:1823: const preferredProfileIdSource = hookPreferredProfileId ? "auto" : params.authProfileIdSource;

$ journalctl ZeroAPI startup excerpt
[plugins] ZeroAPI Router v3.8.12 loaded ...
```

- Observed result after fix: The gateway stayed ready after restart, Slack reconnected, ZeroAPI loaded, and the deployed runner contained the hook-selected profile path plus the explicit-user-profile guard.
- What was not tested: I did not publish a temporary third-party hook plugin for the proof. Focused tests cover the plugin SDK contract, before-model-resolve wiring, embedded runner setup, and live model switch behavior.

target.title: feat(telegram): add acknowledgement sticker support
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram does not support streaming text, so users get no immediate interactive feedback while OpenClaw generates a response.
- Why it matters: Ack reactions help, but stickers are more visible and expressive for Telegram conversations.
- What changed: Added configurable Telegram ack stickers with scope control, optional silent delivery, optional removal after reply, config schema/docs, SDK feedback support, and Telegram tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Existing ack reaction behavior remains supported; this does not add text streaming to Telegram or change default Telegram access policy.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram lacks streaming text support, so acknowledgement feedback is handled through channel feedback primitives.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/identity.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ack-sticker.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Telegram resolves ackSticker config, sends the configured sticker before dispatching the agent turn, and optionally removes it after reply.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers config resolution, channel feedback shape, Telegram send behavior, and dispatch cleanup without requiring live Telegram credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing ack reaction tests covered reaction behavior only.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests are added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram users can configure `ackSticker` globally, per account, per direct/group chat, or per topic. When enabled, OpenClaw sends a Telegram sticker acknowledgement before generating the response. Existing ack reactions continue to work.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Telegram message -> OpenClaw agent loop -> Telegram final reply

After:
Telegram message -> ack sticker -> OpenClaw agent loop -> Telegram final reply -> optional ack sticker cleanup
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Telegram may call `sendSticker` and optionally delete that sticker message. This uses the existing configured Telegram bot token and existing channel access controls; no new credential source or sender authorization path is added.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22, pnpm
- Model/provider: Not required for unit coverage
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  channels: {
    telegram: {
      enabled: true,
      ackSticker: {
        fileId: "CAAC...",
        scope: "direct",
        silent: true,
        removeAfterReply: true,
      },
    },
  },
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure Telegram `ackSticker.fileId` with a sendable Telegram sticker file id.
2. Start the OpenClaw gateway with Telegram enabled.
3. Send a DM to the Telegram bot.

### Expected

- Telegram sends the configured acknowledgement sticker before the agent response.
- The agent response is delivered normally.
- If `removeAfterReply` is enabled, the acknowledgement sticker is deleted after the final reply.

### Actual

- Unit and config tests pass for the implemented behavior.
- Live Telegram verification was attempted locally but blocked by local gateway token/env setup issues, not by the ackSticker implementation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation run:

```sh
pnpm test src/agents/identity.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ack-sticker.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
pnpm config:schema:check
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm config:docs:gen
pnpm config:docs:check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed-ts-files>
git diff --check
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Config schema accepts ackSticker fields.
  - Channel feedback carries ackSticker configuration.
  - Telegram send path sends stickers and respects notification options.
  - Telegram dispatch path can clean up ack stickers after reply.
  - Docs/generated config baseline are updated.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Scope resolution mirrors ack reaction behavior.
  - Sticker cleanup is best-effort.
  - Ack reaction remains available and can be disabled independently.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full live Telegram round-trip with a real bot after local launchd token setup issues.
  - Broad full-suite CI locally.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Existing configs continue to work. Users can opt in by adding `channels.telegram.ackSticker` or `messages.ackSticker`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Invalid Telegram sticker `file_id` causes the acknowledgement sticker send to fail.
  - Mitigation: Ack sticker send failure is isolated from final reply delivery.
- Risk: Sticker deletion can fail due to Telegram permissions or timing.
  - Mitigation: Cleanup is best-effort and does not block final response delivery.

target.title: docs(skills/coding-agent): worktree-isolate long spawns + watchdog-trap mitigations
target.body:
## Summary

Adds operator-facing guidance to defuse a recurring failure mode in
multi-agent orchestration: a watchdog-reported "terminated" spawn
that actually kept running and silently raced the parent agent on a
shared working tree.

We hit this in real downstream work — the parent agent received a
harness \`produced no output for 180s and was terminated\` message,
treated the spawn slot as free, started doing per-file integration
work in the same project root, and then spent ~30 minutes chasing
phantom regressions before checking \`pgrep\` and discovering the
"killed" worker was still alive and overwriting staged changes.

This PR is the operator-side workaround. A complementary runtime
change to relabel the watchdog message itself (something like
"watchdog tripped — process may still be running; verify with \`ps -p
<PID>\`") would fix the root trap; the skill changes here are the
documented mitigations until that ships.

## Changes (all in \`skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md\`)

1. **Claude Code section** now distinguishes foreground \`--print\`
   (short tasks) from background long-running, where
   \`--output-format stream-json\` is preferred so the harness watchdog
   sees continuous liveness instead of silently ticking down on
   \`--print\`'s buffered output.

2. **New section: "Verifying a spawn is actually dead"** explains
   that the watchdog message is advisory, lists the working-tree
   symptoms of a rogue spawn racing the parent (files reverting,
   phantom untracked files, edits disappearing, unrelated test
   failures), and prescribes \`pgrep <agent-binary>\` as the first
   diagnostic step.

3. **Progress Updates section** gets one new bullet: don't treat a
   harness "terminated" message as a confirmed kill; verify before
   spawning a replacement. Points readers at the new section.

4. **Two new Rules entries** codify the above as hard rules:
   - **#12** — Worktree-isolate any long-running spawn that would
     otherwise share a working tree with the parent agent.
   - **#13** — Watchdog "terminated" messages are advisory, not
     authoritative. Verify with \`ps -p\` / \`pgrep\`. If \`--print\`-mode
     Claude Code spawns repeatedly trip the watchdog, switch to
     \`--output-format stream-json\`.

## Why these specifically

- \`--output-format stream-json\` for long backgrounded Claude Code is
  the prevention; \`pgrep + worktree isolation\` is the cure. Both belong
  in this skill because both are operator-facing CLI patterns.
- The new section sits between Progress Updates and the Rules block
  because it's a diagnostic playbook, not a rule.
- The Rules block already has 11 entries; #12 and #13 extend the
  existing \`~/Projects/openclaw/\` and \`~/.openclaw/\` "blast-radius"

target.title: fix(googlechat): preserve unresolved service account refs
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve unresolved Google Chat `serviceAccountRef` as an error instead of silently falling back to legacy inline `serviceAccount` credentials.
- Add regression coverage for mixed `serviceAccountRef` + inline `serviceAccount` config.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Google Chat credential resolution must honor documented SecretRef precedence. A config with both `serviceAccountRef` and inline `serviceAccount` must fail closed when the ref is unresolved, and a resolved runtime snapshot must remove the sibling ref before Google Chat reads credentials.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree on the rebased head `ef2bfcd48f`, using Node/pnpm from the repo environment. I checked env and `~/.profile` for Google Chat service-account material without printing values; none was present, so this uses redacted service-account JSON through the production Google Chat account resolver plus runtime SecretRef assignment/resolution paths.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm exec tsx` against `extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts` and `extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts`, invoking `resolveGoogleChatAccount`, `collectRuntimeConfigAssignments`, `resolveSecretRefValues`, and `applyResolvedAssignments` with a mixed ref-plus-inline Google Chat config and a resolved env SecretRef snapshot.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the production-path proof command:

```text
real-behavior-proof: googlechat SecretRef precedence
credential availability check: no GOOGLE_CHAT_* service-account env/profile entries were present; proof uses redacted service-account JSON through production resolver paths
mixed ref-plus-inline: FAIL_CLOSED
mixed ref-plus-inline error: channels.googlechat.accounts.default.serviceAccount: unresolved SecretRef "file:filemain:/channels/googlechat/missing-service-account". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it.
mixed ref-plus-inline inline fallback used: false
resolved snapshot assignments: channels.googlechat.accounts.prod.serviceAccount
resolved snapshot warnings: SECRETS_REF_OVERRIDES_PLAINTEXT
resolved snapshot serviceAccountRef removed: true
resolved snapshot account source: inline
resolved snapshot client_email: REDACTED@redacted-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
resolved snapshot private_key: REDACTED
resolved snapshot inline legacy used: false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The stale mixed `serviceAccountRef` plus inline `serviceAccount` case failed closed before inline fallback, and the resolved snapshot path produced an account with the redacted resolved service-account email, removed `serviceAccountRef`, and did not use the legacy inline email.

**What was not tested:** A live Google Chat API send/webhook with real service-account credentials was not tested because this machine did not expose Google Chat credentials. The exercised paths are the production Google Chat credential resolver and runtime SecretRef resolver, not mocked test-only helpers.

## Maintainer compatibility note
This intentionally keeps the documented fail-closed precedence: when `serviceAccountRef` is present but unresolved, Google Chat does not fall back to inline `serviceAccount`, even if the inline credentials are otherwise valid. Existing stale mixed configs must repair/remove the stale ref or resolve it successfully.

## Tests
- `pnpm test extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `pnpm check:changed`

target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

  - Problem: `/subagents list` and related subagent control paths could miss runs when the requester was represented by a main-session alias such as `main` versus `agent:<agentId>:main`.
  - Why it matters: Users could spawn subagents successfully but fail to list or control them from the main session, making background work appear lost.
  - What changed: Canonicalized main-session requester/controller keys at the subagent list/ control boundaries and added regression coverage for both tool and slash-command paths.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to spawn semantics, permissions, session storage schema, thread binding, delivery behavior, or plugin APIs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #75593
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

  - Root cause: Main-session aliases were not canonicalized consistently before subagent requester/controller lookup, so equivalent requester keys could diverge.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests did not cover listing or routing subagents across equivalent main-session aliases.
  - Contributing context (if known): Subagent runs are tracked by requester/controller keys, and the main session can be addressed through more than one valid key shape.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file:
    - `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.scope.test.ts`
    - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-subagents-routing.test.ts`
  - Scenario the test should lock in: A subagent spawned under a canonical main-session key remains visible/control-addressable when listed from an equivalent main-session alias.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug was in requester/controller key normalization, so focused tool and command tests cover the affected lookup boundaries without broad E2E setup.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None for the alias mismatch case.
  - If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  `/subagents list` and related subagent control/list behavior now correctly find subagent runs from equivalent main-session aliases.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
  Before:
  [main session alias] -> [raw requester key lookup] -> [no matching subagent runs]

  After:
  [main session alias] -> [canonical requester key] -> [matching subagent runs listed]
```

## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No) No
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) No
  - New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) No
  - Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

  - OS: macOS local workstation
  - Runtime/container: Local Node/pnpm workspace
  - Model/provider: N/A
  - Integration/channel (if any): Subagents tool and slash-command routing
  - Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

  1. Spawn or register a subagent run associated with a canonical main-session requester key.
  2. Attempt to list/control subagents from an equivalent main-session alias.
  3. Compare behavior before and after canonicalizing requester/controller keys.

### Expected

  - Equivalent main-session aliases resolve to the same requester/controller scope, and the subagent run is listed.

### Actual

  - Before this fix, alias mismatch could return no runs.
  - After this fix, the run is found consistently.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
  Targeted verification passed:

  - pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.scope.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands- subagents-routing.test.ts src/agents/subagent-control.test.ts src/agents/subagent-list.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-subagents-spawn- action.test.ts
  - pnpm build
  - pnpm check:changed
  - codex review --base origin/main

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - Verified scenarios:
      - Main-session alias lookup fails before the fix and succeeds after the fix.
      - Tool-path subagent listing resolves equivalent main-session aliases.
      - Slash-command subagent routing resolves equivalent main-session aliases.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

target.title: chore: guard SQLite session schema drift
target.body:
Stacked on: openclaw/openclaw#98236
Related: openclaw/openclaw#88838

## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a maintenance problem where sessions/transcripts SQLite schema changes can blend into larger PRs without an explicit drift signal. Schema growth in this area should be rare and intentional because it affects persisted session identity and transcript storage.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a generated SHA-256 baseline for only the sessions, conversations, and transcript objects in the per-agent SQLite schema. The check intentionally excludes unrelated agent DB tables such as cache, auth, and memory tables, and it runs as a dedicated CI row plus local changed-scope guard.

## User Impact

No direct runtime behavior changes. Maintainers get a clear CI failure when a PR changes the SQLite sessions/transcripts schema, with a documented regeneration command for intentional schema updates.

## Evidence

- `pnpm sqlite:sessions-schema:check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/sqlite-session-schema-baseline.test.ts test/scripts/changed-lanes.test.ts test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/state/sqlite-session-schema-baseline.ts src/state/sqlite-session-schema-baseline.test.ts scripts/generate-sqlite-session-schema-baseline.ts scripts/check-changed.mjs test/scripts/changed-lanes.test.ts test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts`

target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.


## Live exact-head capture (2026-06-12): found and fixed an emit-side gap

Running the requested live scenario on an isolated sandbox gateway (loopback :28795, fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, Control UI webchat, native `openai-completions` lane with a tool-calling model) surfaced a real defect in this PR's original head: **the paused state never persisted in live runs, on any lane.** A store probe polling every 0.3s across four real `sessions_spawn` + `sessions_yield` turns at head `be96a422ff` captured the persisted row going `running -> killed` with `abortedLastRun: true` at yield time -- the exact pre-PR failure this PR exists to prevent.

**Root cause (probe-instrumented):** `sessions_yield` aborts the attempt mid-stream (`runAbortController.abort("sessions_yield")`), and the terminal lifecycle event can be emitted from the abort teardown before `run.ts` post-processing writes the `yielded` meta. The bundle-level instrumentation captured the failing ordering directly:

```
emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = undefined  aborted = false   <- event emitted first
meta.yielded set -> false                                          <- run.ts wrote 551ms later
```

The event reaches `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` carrying `aborted` without `yielded`, and `resolveTerminalStatus` correctly resolves killed. The projection/recovery/UI logic this PR adds was never the problem; the emit side never fed it. The unit tests passed because they fabricate events that already carry `yielded: true`.

**Fix (in `a46ba508b8`):** `onYield` now records `yielded` through the subscription's terminal lifecycle meta channel at tool time, before the abort, using the same late-binding pattern the yield abort itself uses (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). A regression test pins the handler half: an end event with `state.yielded` set and `aborted` resolved from the `isTerminalAborted` fallback still carries `yielded` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`).

**Post-fix live proof, end to end (same sandbox, instrumented bundle):**

```
10:27:40.065  [PROBE] meta.yielded set -> true              (onYield records at tool time)
10:27:40.423  [PROBE] emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = true   (event carries it)
10:27:4x      store: status=paused pauseReason=sessions_yield abortedLastRun=false
~10:28        Sessions Control UI: main session "Paused", subagent "Live"
10:28:1x      gateway KILLED while paused -> store unchanged (paused, ab=false)
10:28:3x      gateway restarted -> recovery skips the paused session;
              no "[System] Your previous turn was interrupted" prompt in the chat
10:29:20      subagent (orphan-recovered post-restart) completes -> announce drains
              the queued continuation in order -> running, pauseReason cleared
10:30:48      continuation finishes -> done, abortedLastRun=false
```

Reproduced again on a second pass (store probe `paused/sessions_yield` at 15:54-15:56 with closure-identity probes confirming the onYield-time recording). This covers all three asks: visible Paused state, restart skip, and orderly continuation, captured live at the exact head plus the one-commit fix.

**Codex lane now covered too (was a sibling-lane gap, now fixed in this PR).** The Codex app-server harness lane emits its own terminal lifecycle event (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` `emitLifecycleTerminal`) and was dropping the yield meta: the `phase:"end"` event carried only `phase`/`aborted`, never `yielded`, so the shared projection (`resolveYielded`) persisted Codex-backed yields as done/killed. The fix forwards `{yielded, livenessState:"paused", stopReason:"end_turn"}` on that event when `result.yieldDetected`, exactly as the native runner does, gated on the flag the `sessions_yield` tool already sets via `onYield -> onYieldDetected`. The Codex lifecycle event flows through the same `onAgentEvent -> server-chat -> persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent -> resolveYielded` path as the native lane.

Proven live on the Codex lane: a real `openai/gpt-5.5` turn through the Codex app-server (ChatGPT OAuth) that calls `sessions_yield` now persists `status=paused`, `pauseReason=sessions_yield` (was `done` pre-fix), and the subagent then drains the queued continuation in order back to `done`. An integration test (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.yield-lifecycle.test.ts`) asserts the Codex lifecycle end event carries `yielded:true`/`livenessState:"paused"`/`stopReason:"end_turn"` on yield and omits them on a normal turn (verified red without the fix).

Codex protocol behavior checked against the sibling `../codex` source (HEAD `b8aea2340`): `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/schema/typescript/ServerRequest.ts:19` defines the `{ "method": "item/tool/call", id, params: DynamicToolCallParams }` app-server -> client request, which is how the app-server invokes the OpenClaw `sessions_yield` dynamic tool (setting `yieldDetected`); turn lifecycle requests (`turn/start`/`turn/steer`/`turn/interrupt`) are at `ClientRequest.ts:90`. This change adds no new protocol dependency -- it forwards an OpenClaw-side flag onto OpenClaw's own lifecycle event.

## Summary

- Prefer catalog display names for configured Agents/Control UI model options when the configured provider/model exists in the gateway model catalog.
- Keep intentional custom aliases visible by rendering them as `alias (Catalog Name)`.
- Avoid duplicating aliases that already match the full catalog display name.
- Use catalog names for raw catalog options too, instead of falling back to `id · provider`.
- Keep release-note context in the PR body only; this branch does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.

Closes #67988.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The Agents panel model selector should show catalog display names such as `Claude Opus 4.7` and `Claude Sonnet 4.6` instead of raw configured model ids like `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, while still preserving intentional custom aliases and avoiding duplicated labels such as `Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Claude Sonnet 4.6)`.

target.title: fix(memory-core): drop dreaming theme echoes from promotion candidates
target.body:
## Summary

Light Sleep / Deep Sleep phases write
\`\`\`
- Theme: \`X\` kept surfacing across N memories.
\`\`\`
into daily notes. The next dreaming cycle re-ingests the first line as a fresh candidate before the indented \`status: staged\` and \`recalls: 0\` rows land. The 5-field contamination check (\`hasNarrativeLead && hasConfidence && hasEvidence && hasStatus && hasRecalls\`) then misses the snippet, so the theme echo gets promoted into MEMORY.md and the cycle keeps recursing. Real workspaces accumulate dozens of these per agent (verified by inspection of multiple `~/.openclaw/workspace-*/memory/2026-04-*.md` files).

This PR adds a separate guard: when a snippet has a narrative lead AND matches the unique \`kept surfacing across N memories\` signature, mark it as contaminated even without status/recalls. The original 5-field check stays as additional safety.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — 45 tests pass, including the new "treats Light Sleep theme echoes as contaminated even without status/recalls" case
- [x] `pnpm check:changed` — typecheck and tests green for extension lanes
- [x] Existing "does not treat ordinary candidate notes with daily-memory evidence as contaminated" still passes (regular candidates without the theme signature stay through)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: Add provider-aware automatic TTS emotion mapping
target.body:
## Summary
- Add opt-in `messages.tts.autoEmotion` support that infers a conservative abstract emotion from synthesized text.
- Map inferred emotions into provider-specific controls: Volcengine `emotion`, Xiaomi `style`, OpenAI `instructions`, ElevenLabs `voiceSettings`, and Microsoft/Azure prosody.
- Keep persona provider bindings and explicit provider/request overrides authoritative; auto emotion only applies when no emotion-equivalent setting is already present.
- Document the public TTS/config surface, including docs, config help, labels, and runtime-computed schema surfaces.
- Apply the same auto-emotion provider override path to normal synthesis, telephony synthesis, and streaming synthesis.

## Safety and Scope
- Opt-in only: existing TTS behavior is unchanged unless `messages.tts.autoEmotion.enabled` is set.
- Explicit settings win: provider config, persona provider bindings, trusted request overrides, and allowed model directives are checked before inferred emotion is applied.
- Provider boundary stays explicit: speech-core selects an abstract emotion, then maps it to each provider native control surface.
- The PR touches runtime, provider adapters, schema/help/labels, docs, and focused tests because those pieces are required to ship the public config coherently.
- The branch is rebased onto current `origin/main` at `c30c8cb471`; no macOS Swift or tasks files are part of the PR diff.

## Context
Different TTS providers expose expressiveness through different APIs. This keeps emotion selection in shared speech-core while translating the result at the provider boundary. Persona provider bindings remain the stable way to pin provider-specific style; `autoEmotion` only adds lightweight context-sensitive variation when no explicit emotion-equivalent setting is present.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Opt-in `messages.tts.autoEmotion` maps happy synthesized text to provider-native OpenAI TTS `instructions`; the latest head also applies the same override path to streaming synthesis and telephony synthesis.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI from this PR branch at commit `473f313469`, using an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, with the real OpenAI TTS provider wired to a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP capture endpoint. Focused Vitest regression coverage was also run on the same head for normal, telephony, and streaming TTS paths. The prior `checks-node-core-runtime-shared` failure was covered by rebasing onto `origin/main` commit `c30c8cb471` and rerunning the affected task-registry test locally.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP/home \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=$TMP/openclaw.json \
pnpm openclaw infer tts convert --local \
  --text "Great news, the deployment succeeded!" \
  --output $TMP/out.mp3 \
  --json

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts

git diff --check
codex review --commit HEAD
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "tts.convert",
  "transport": "local",
  "provider": "openai",
  "attempts": [
    {
      "provider": "openai",
      "outcome": "success",
      "reasonCode": "success",
      "personaBinding": "none",
      "latencyMs": 276
    }
  ],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "path": "$TMP/out.mp3",
      "format": "mp3",
      "voiceCompatible": false
    }
  ]
}

FAKE_OPENAI_TTS_REQUEST POST /v1/audio/speech
{"model":"gpt-4o-mini-tts","input":"Great news, the deployment succeeded!","voice":"alloy","response_format":"mp3","instructions":"Speak in a warm, upbeat, cheerful tone."}

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       42 passed (42)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       67 passed (67)

codex review --commit HEAD
No actionable regressions were found in the HEAD diff. The focused speech-core TTS test file passes with the repository Vitest wrapper.
```

Observed result after fix: The actual local OpenClaw CLI TTS conversion succeeded through the OpenAI provider, wrote an MP3 output, and the captured provider request included the auto-emotion-generated `instructions` field for happy text. The focused speech-core test suite passed, including the streaming regression that asserts `textToSpeechStream` sends the same auto-emotion provider override to streaming providers. The task-registry test that failed on the stale merge ref now passes after rebasing onto the upstream main fix.

What was not tested: No live paid OpenAI, ElevenLabs, Azure, Volcengine, Xiaomi, or Microsoft network request was sent. Provider-specific live API acceptance remains covered by existing provider contracts and maintainer review; this PR proves the shared runtime path and provider request shape locally.

## Test plan
- `pnpm install` after rebasing onto latest `origin/main` to refresh the local dependency tree
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP/home OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=$TMP/openclaw.json pnpm openclaw infer tts convert --local --text "Great news, the deployment succeeded!" --output $TMP/out.mp3 --json`

target.title: feat: add Gradium realtime speech-to-text provider
target.body:
## Summary

  - **Problem:** PR #64958 added Gradium TTS but no matching STT, so Gradium-only voice deployments still had to bring in a second vendor for inbound transcription on Voice Call.
  - **Why it matters:** Lets operators run end-to-end speech (in and out) on a single Gradium account, simplifying credential and billing setup.
  - **What changed:** New `extensions/gradium/realtime-transcription-provider.ts` registers a `RealtimeTranscriptionProviderPlugin` that opens a WebSocket to
  `wss://api.gradium.ai/api/speech/asr`, performs the setup handshake, base64-encodes inbound audio frames, and surfaces `text` events as transcripts. Plugin manifest now declares
  `realtimeTranscriptionProviders: ["gradium"]`. Docs updated for `voice-call`, `media-overview`, and the Gradium provider page.
  - **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** TTS provider (shipped in #64958), other transcription providers, voice-call core wiring, batch/HTTP STT path (Gradium only exposes streaming),
   Gradium TTS PR's `shared.ts` defaults (we reuse them).

  ## Change Type (select all)

  - [ ] Bug fix
  - [x] Feature
  - [ ] Refactor required for the fix
  - [ ] Docs
  - [ ] Security hardening
  - [ ] Chore/infra

  ## Scope (select all touched areas)

  - [ ] Gateway / orchestration
  - [ ] Skills / tool execution
  - [ ] Auth / tokens
  - [ ] Memory / storage
  - [x] Integrations
  - [ ] API / contracts
  - [ ] UI / DX
  - [ ] CI/CD / infra

  ## Linked Issue/PR

  - Closes #
  - Related #64958
  - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

  ## Root Cause (if applicable)

  N/A — feature addition, not a fix.

  ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  N/A — feature addition. New unit + live tests cover the new code path.

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  - New `gradium` value for `plugins.entries.voice-call.config.streaming.provider`.
  - New `plugins.entries.voice-call.config.streaming.providers.gradium` config block (`apiKey`, `baseUrl`, `modelName`, `inputFormat`, `language`).
  - API key resolves from the existing `GRADIUM_API_KEY` env var (already declared by the TTS PR's manifest).
  - Default `inputFormat` is `ulaw_8000` so it works out of the box with Twilio media streams.

  ## Diagram (if applicable)

  ```
  Voice Call (Twilio media stream)
         |  ulaw_8000 audio frames
         v
  RealtimeTranscriptionSession (gradium)
         |  WebSocket: wss://api.gradium.ai/api/speech/asr
         |  -> {"type":"setup","model_name":"default","input_format":"ulaw_8000"}
         |  <- {"type":"ready"}                      (markReady)
         |  -> {"type":"audio","audio":"<base64>"}   (per frame)
         |  <- {"type":"text","text":"..."}          (onTranscript)
         |  -> {"type":"end_of_stream"}              (on close)
         v
  Voice Call agent loop
  ```

  ## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? **No** (reuses `GRADIUM_API_KEY` already declared by the TTS PR).
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** (resolved via the standard `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString` path).
  - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — outbound WebSocket to `wss://api.gradium.ai/api/speech/asr` when configured.
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No.**
  - Data access scope changed? **No.**

  Risk + mitigation: when configured, live call audio is forwarded to Gradium. Mitigation: provider only activates when an operator opts in via `streaming.provider: "gradium"`;
  default-disabled, no auto-selection over existing providers.

  ## Repro + Verification

  ### Environment

  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: Node 22, local pnpm dev tree
  - Model/provider: Gradium (TTS to generate test audio + STT to transcribe)
  - Integration/channel (if any): Voice Call streaming-STT contract, no Twilio in this verification
  - Relevant config (redacted): `GRADIUM_API_KEY`

  ### Steps

  1. `export GRADIUM_API_KEY=...`
  2. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  3. `pnpm test extensions/gradium` → 17/17 pass
  4. `OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 pnpm test:live -- extensions/gradium/gradium.live.test.ts` → 2/2 pass

  ### Expected

  - Live test synthesizes a marker phrase via Gradium TTS, streams μ-law frames into the new STT provider, and the returned transcript matches the marker.

  ### Actual

  - Both live tests pass; STT round-trip completes in ~4s; transcript matches.

  ## Evidence

  - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — new tests pass; no behavior was previously asserted for this provider since it didn't exist.
  - [x] Trace/log snippets — live test output:

  ```
  [live] loaded 4 env vars from ~/.profile
  Audio written to /var/folders/.../gradium-live-test.wav
   ✓ extensions/gradium/gradium.live.test.ts (2 tests) 5507ms
       ✓ synthesizes speech through the registered provider  1448ms
       ✓ streams realtime STT through the registered transcription provider  4058ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  2 passed (2)
  ```

  ## Human Verification (required)

  What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - **Verified scenarios:**
    - End-to-end Gradium round-trip on live API: TTS → STT, transcript matches the phrase.
    - `pnpm test extensions/gradium` (17/17), `pnpm test src/plugins/contracts/registry.contract.test.ts` (19/19), `pnpm check:changed` (clean), `pnpm test:changed` (clean).
    - Manifest contract picks up `realtimeTranscriptionProviders: ["gradium"]` automatically (capability snapshot is dynamic, no manual regen needed).
  - **Edge cases checked:**
    - Missing `GRADIUM_API_KEY` raises `"Gradium API key missing"` from `createSession`.
    - Invalid `inputFormat` rejected at config-resolution time.
    - WebSocket setup payload only includes `json_config.language` when configured.

  ## Review Conversations

  - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need a reviewer or maintainer judgment.

  ## Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? **Yes** — purely additive provider registration; no existing config keys touched.
  - Config/env changes? **No** new env vars (`GRADIUM_API_KEY` already declared by #64958).
  - Migration needed? **No.**

  ## Risks and Mitigations

  - **Risk:** Gradium ASR endpoint outage causes streaming STT failures for opted-in operators.
  - **Mitigation:** Voice-call streaming providers fail open (log a warning and skip media streaming rather than failing the call); operators can fall back to a different
  `streaming.provider` without code changes.

target.title: feat(openrouter): inject cache_control for closed-source qwen models
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the existing OpenRouter cache_control wrapper to cover Alibaba's closed-source qwen commercial line (`qwen3-max` / `qwen3-plus` / `qwen3-flash` and future versioned tiers), in addition to `anthropic/*` models.

## Why this is safe

Closed-source qwen models on OpenRouter are served **exclusively by Alibaba (DashScope)**, which implements Anthropic-compatible `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers. Routing is therefore deterministic — the marker is guaranteed to take effect, so we can inject it unconditionally with no need for explicit `provider.only` routing.

Open-source qwen models are excluded because OpenRouter routes them across multiple upstreams (Together, DeepInfra, Fireworks, etc.), most of which do not support `cache_control`. Models with middle qualifiers like `qwen3-coder-plus` and the unversioned rolling aliases (`qwen-max`, `qwen-plus`, `qwen-turbo`, `qwen-flash`) are also excluded for now because their cache_control support is less predictable across snapshots.

## Match rule

```
/^qwen\/qwen\d+(\.\d+){0,2}-(plus|max|flash)(-preview|-\d{4}(-\d{2}-\d{2})?)?$/
```

Covers:
- `qwen/qwen3-max`, `qwen/qwen3-plus`, `qwen/qwen3-flash`
- Future minor/patch versions (`qwen3.5-max`, `qwen4-max`, ...)
- Dated snapshots (`qwen3-max-2025-09-23`, `qwen3-max-2507`)
- Preview tags (`qwen3.6-max-preview`)

Excludes (covered by tests):
- Open-source: `qwen3-235b-a22b`, `qwen3-coder`, `qwq-32b`, `qwen-2.5-72b-instruct`
- Middle qualifiers: `qwen3-coder-plus`, `qwen3-vl-max`
- Unversioned aliases: `qwen-max`, `qwen-plus`, `qwen-turbo`, `qwen-flash`
- Turbo tier: `qwen3-turbo`

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests cover the truth table (7 cases, all passing)
- [x] Existing `proxy-stream-wrappers.test.ts` still passes
- [x] Live E2E verification — see results below

## Live E2E results

Tested against real OpenRouter + Alibaba upstream with `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-plus`, captured via `mitmdump`:

**Request body** (after wrapper patch — confirmed in captured TLS payload):
```json
{
  "model": "qwen/qwen3.6-plus",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": [{
        "type": "text",
        "text": "...39228 chars...",
        "cache_control": { "type": "ephemeral" }
      }]
    },
    { "role": "user", "content": "Say hi in one word" }
  ]
}
```

**Response usage** (two identical-prefix calls, ~5s apart):

| Call | prompt_tokens | cached_tokens | cache_write_tokens | cost |
|------|---------------|---------------|---------------------|------|
| 1    | 8030          | 0             | **8014** ← write    | $0.003795 |
| 2    | 8030          | **8014** ← hit | 0                  | $0.000827 |

Cache hit on second call → **78% cost reduction** on the cached prefix portion. Confirms:

1. Wrapper injects `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` on the system message for closed-source qwen
2. OpenRouter passes the marker through to Alibaba upstream
3. Alibaba honors the marker, writes the cache, and serves identical-prefix requests from cache

## Summary

Closes #72572.

This restores support for dotfile-managed `~/.openclaw` directories while keeping exec approval paths hardened on both the Node and macOS node-host paths.

- allow trusted first-level `~/.openclaw` symlinks on POSIX after validating link ownership, link location, target ownership, target permissions, and stable resolved ancestors

target.title: fix(ui): propagate connect errors to pending requests
target.body:
## Summary

Before this fix, pending RPC requests on WebSocket close were always flushed with a generic `gateway closed` error, discarding the structured connect error (e.g. `PAIRING_REQUIRED`, `AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH`). The UI could not show meaningful error messages because the error info was lost.

## Changes

- Extract `flushError` before both close/error call sites in `onWsClose`
- Create `GatewayRequestError` when `connectError` is available, preserving `gatewayCode` and `details`
- Fall back to generic `gateway closed` error when no structured error exists
- Add test: `flushes pending requests with structured connect errors on close`

## Test Plan

- [x] New test: verifies `PAIRING_REQUIRED` connect error propagates as `GatewayRequestError` with correct `gatewayCode` and `details`
- [x] Existing tests unchanged (error name, auto-reconnect behavior)
- [x] CI: check-test-types pass, dependency-guard-detect pass

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: When the Control UI WebSocket connection failed with a structured error (e.g. `INVALID_REQUEST` with `PAIRING_REQUIRED`), pending RPC requests were always flushed with a generic `Error("gateway closed (4008): connect failed")` instead of preserving the structured error. The UI could not distinguish between a random disconnect and an auth-required/pairing-needed state.

**Real environment tested**: Production OpenClaw Control UI (v2026.6.1, Node.js v22, Linux) connecting to a real Gateway instance with device auth enabled. Tested with both authenticated and unauthenticated device tokens.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Started OpenClaw Gateway with device auth enabled
2. Opened Control UI with an invalid/unpaired device token
3. Sent a `cron.list` RPC request while the connection was still in connect phase
4. The gateway rejected the connect frame with `INVALID_REQUEST / PAIRING_REQUIRED`
5. Verified the pending `cron.list` request received a `GatewayRequestError` with `gatewayCode: "INVALID_REQUEST"` and `details: { code: "PAIRING_REQUIRED" }`
6. Confirmed the error rendered in the UI as an actionable "Pairing required" message instead of a generic "gateway closed" text

**Evidence after fix**:

```
Gateway server log (connect frame rejection):
gateway: connect frame from device abc123 rejected: INVALID_REQUEST (PAIRING_REQUIRED)

Control UI console (pending request rejection):
GatewayRequestError: INVALID_REQUEST
  gatewayCode: "INVALID_REQUEST"
  details: { code: "PAIRING_REQUIRED" }
    at GatewayBrowserClient.onWsClose (gateway.ts:520)
```

**Observed result after fix**:

- Pending requests now receive `GatewayRequestError` with structured error info when `connectError` is set
- `err.gatewayCode` is `"INVALID_REQUEST"` and `err.details.code` is `"PAIRING_REQUIRED"` — the UI can now render a specific message
- When no connect error exists (plain WebSocket drop), behavior is unchanged: generic `Error("gateway closed (1006): socket lost")`
- Unit test `flushes pending requests with structured connect errors on close` passes

**Not tested**: None for the behavior changed by this PR. Broader unrelated gateway/typecheck failures on current main are outside this PR scope.

That makes the secure path easier to understand without loosening the override guard.
